items.title: Van nee voor Pepi tot no voor Sano: de elf uit de Eredivisie die niet vertrokken
items.summary: Na het sluiten van de transfermarkt in alle grote Europese topcompetities, is ook die van Nederland na dinsdag op slot. Het gaat natuurlijk veel over spelers die de Eredivisie hebben versterkt, maar er zijn ook spelers die juist geen transfer hebben gemaakt. VI heeft een elftal samengesteld van zij die niet gingen.

items.title: Kanté joins Fenerbahce from Al Ittihad
items.summary: Fenerbahce have completed the signing of former Leicester and Chelsea midfielder N'Golo Kanté.

items.title: Ajax-target Vicente legt uit waarom hij voor Birmingham City koos
items.summary: Carlos Vicente stond afgelopen winter in de belangstelling van Ajax, maar koos voor Birmingham City. De 26-jarige buitenspeler heeft uitgelegd waarom hij La Liga inruilde voor de hyperambitieuze club uit het Championship.

items.title: Arteta: 'Magical' final is 'best vitamins' for Arsenal
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has described Arsenal 's "magical" achievement in reaching the Carabao Cup Final as "the best vitamins we can put in our bodies."

items.title: Araujo hugs Flick after goal in 'step by step' return
items.summary: Hansi Flick said Barcelona will continue to support Ronald Araújo after the defender netted on his first start for the club since returning from a mental health break against Albacete in the Copa del Rey on Tuesday.

items.title: Benzema on new club Al Hilal: 'Madrid in Asia'
items.summary: Karim Benzema said he wants to bring trophies to his new club Al Hilal, which he called the "Real Madrid in Asia."

items.title: Topspelers Quiz: de hoogste marktwaardes in de voetballerij
items.summary: De miljoenen vlogen de voetballiefhebber weer om de oren in de afgelopen wintertransferperiode, maar welke spelers zijn nu het meeste waard? We zoeken de vijftig waardevolste spelers van dit moment.

items.title: Everton sack Sorensen days after first home WSL win of season
items.summary: Brian Sorensen, who took charge in 2022, is sacked with Everton fourth from bottom of the Women's Super League.

items.title: 'De rest gaat ten onder aan de absurde eisen die aan trainers worden gesteld'
items.summary: Süleyman Öztürk over de zwaarte van het trainersvak.

items.title: Everton sack Brian Sørensen with club languishing near bottom of WSL table
items.summary: Dane leaves after almost four years as head coach Form at Goodison Park has been poor this season Everton Women have sacked their manager Brian Sørensen with the club ninth in the Women’s Super League. The club claimed their first home victory of the season on Sunday to move four points clear of relegation danger but it has not been enough to keep the 45-year-old Dane, in charge since 2022, in his role. Everton’s chief executive, Angus Kinnear, said: “Sunday’s first win of the season at Goodison Park was important, but it has become evident that a change of direction is necessary to ensure the progression on the pitch we are all striving for.” Continue reading...

items.title: How did transfer window impact Championship promotion race?
items.summary: The January window is shut and the race for the Premier League is well and truly on.

items.title: Erdogan op de bres: Turkse president schoot Fenerbahçe te hulp bij transfer Kanté
items.summary: Fenerbahçe heeft zich alsnog weten te versterken met N'Golo Kanté, dankzij de hulp van Recep Tayyip Erdogan. De Turkse president sprong tijdens zijn staatsbezoek in Saoedi-Arabië op de bres, nadat hij weet kreeg van de eerdere afgeketste deal met Al-Ittihad. In een verklaring van Fenerbahçe-voorzitter Sadettin Saran, bedankte de club Erdogan voor het regelen van de transfer.

items.title: 'Out of order!' McCullum hits back at 'pile on' over England behaviour
items.summary: Brendon McCullum has dismissed criticism of England's behaviour as "out of order" and hit out at the perception that he runs a "loose ship".

items.title: Brundle: Piastri will come back with a vengeance in 2026
items.summary: Martin Brundle expects Oscar Piastri to "come back with a vengeance" after narrowly missing out on a maiden Formula 1 title last year.

items.title: Celebrating the most remarkable almost-one-club players in football | The Knowledge
items.summary: Plus: footballers’ weddings on live television, the most successful fictional teams, and more Mail us with your questions and answers “Ian Muir played 95% of his games for Tranmere,” writes Robert Abushal. “One-club players aside, who’s the closest to 100% without being 100%?” One-club men and women are among football’s more celebrated groups, the players who dedicated their entire career to one particular cause. Athletic Club give out the One Club Man and One Club Woman awards each year; the list of recipients include Paolo Maldini, Matthew Le Tissier and Malin Moström. We haven’t included non-league teams, which rules out Paul Scholes (three games for Royton) and Le Tissier (Eastleigh) among others. We’ve also excluded Hamburg legend Uwe Seeler, whose one appearance for Cork Celtic was in a sponsored event. Data on appearances for individual players can vary from source to source, particularly for older players. We made a judgment call in each case, so the figures may only be 99.82% correct. But that’s appropriate for this question, right? Right? Continue reading...

items.title: Outspoken Cristian Romero brings his own form of leadership to Tottenham
items.summary: The Spurs captain is driven by an internal fire and is unafraid of dropping truth bombs on the club’s ownership Cristian Romero had been named as the Tottenham captain, a symbol of a new era, of fresh direction and hope. It was last September, the eve of the club’s Champions League return against Villarreal and it was time for him to speak to the English media. A rare appointment but one that could not be sidestepped given his rise in status. There had to be a few nerves at Spurs because Romero was not exactly the diplomat over the course of the previous season, dropping his truth bombs, the shrapnel flying at the board and ownership, in particular. It would be a bit awkward in parts but Romero got through it. There were no unwanted headlines. Continue reading...

items.title: Kante joins Fenerbahce after deal revival
items.summary: N'Golo Kante joins Fenerbahce from Al-Ittihad after issues over documentation appeared to threaten deal.

items.title: Quadruple-chasing Arsenal can dream a micromanaged dream | Barney Ronay
items.summary: After prevailing in a gristly physical ballet with Chelsea, Arsenal’s season has now reached a point of pre-ignition Zamina mina. Waka, waka, hé-hé. By the end of this gruelling, bruising, deep tissue ache of a football match, it felt as though the opening 96 minutes had been staged simply as an extended tease for a startlingly carefree final 30 seconds. Up to that point Arsenal and Chelsea had produced something that felt like the football equivalent of having your eyes descaled with a wire brush. This was a dense, gristly kind of physical ballet. Johan Cruyff once said that in football the clock is never your friend. It’s either moving too fast or too slowly. Here the clock didn’t really seem to move at all, or to be going backwards. The clock hated everyone. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Playing with fire' catches up with 10-man Hearts - McInnes
items.summary: Head coach Derek McInnes concedes that Premiership leaders Hearts were punished for "playing with fire" as a third red card in five matches contributes to a 1-0 defeat at St Mirren.

items.title: 'Years in the making' - are Arsenal ready to shed 'nearly-men' tag?
items.summary: Arsenal have moved a step closer to winning their first major silverware since 2020 by beating Chelsea 4-2 on aggregate in the Carabao Cup semi-final.
